coreJobStatement: Design, manufacture, and deliver powered earthmoving, lifting, compaction, and drilling equipment that repositions terrain, lifts loads, and prepares ground at construction, mining, and infrastructure job sites [SRC: VN_construction_machinery]
productJobStatement: Help Steel Input Materials selection to provide constant-cross-section steel structural profiles at specified dimensional tolerances (+-0.02 to +-0.5 mm), strength grade (S355-S690), and optional surface hardness (>=55 HRC) for direct integration into excavator boom/arm subassemblies, frame structures, and sliding interface components
anchorLevel: L4 (component — structural profile within steel input materials)
lPath: L7 Construction Equipment Manufacturing Ecosystem > L6a Structural Steel Fabrication > L5 Steel Input Materials > L4 Boom/Arm Chord Profiles, Slide Guide Channels, Pivot Pins, Wear Strips

segments.name: New platform development
segments.characteristics: First-article geometry undefined; design freedom; 6-18 month tooling lead accepted
segments.targetabilityReason: All alternatives compete — plate, hollow sections, forgings, near-net profiles
segments.circumstance: First-article geometry undefined; design freedom; 6-18 month tooling lead accepted

segments.name: High-volume serial production
segments.characteristics: Geometry frozen; annual call-off contracts; zero tooling lead time acceptable
segments.circumstance: Geometry frozen; annual call-off contracts; zero tooling lead time acceptable
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — incumbent wins; competitor = re-qualification cost

segments.name: Aftermarket / wear part replacement
segments.characteristics: Condition-based replacement; needs standard geometry stock
segments.targetabilityReason: Standard geometry stock; bolt-on aftermarket plates compete
segments.circumstance: Condition-based replacement (inspect every 250 hr; replace at 50% wear); needs standard geometry stock
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — bolt-on aftermarket plates and polymer liners compete

segments.name: Platform redesign / lightweighting
segments.characteristics: Boom/arm geometry revised to reduce weight; S690QL upgrade path
segments.targetabilityReason: High-strength plate (S690/Weldox) competes with near-net profile
segments.circumstance: Boom/arm geometry revised to reduce weight; S690QL / Weldox 700 upgrade path
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — high-strength plate and near-net profile in upgraded grade compete

alternatives.name: Near-net-shape precision profiles (cold-drawn / hot-rolled)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Tooling NRE 5-15K EUR; 6-12 wk first-article lead time; min 100 kg batch; geometry fixed to 2D constant cross-section

alternatives.name: Laser/plasma-cut flat plate + weld fabrication
alternatives.tradeoffs: Multiple weld passes increase distortion risk; 40-60% material waste vs. near-net; weld sequence controls geometry; dominant at ~60% share

alternatives.name: Standard structural hollow sections (SHS/RHS from service center)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Catalog geometry only; limited cross-section optimization; standard tolerance +-1-2 mm; no IH option; ~15% share

alternatives.name: Hot forgings (complex node elements)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Die NRE 50-150K EUR; 6-18 month qualification; near-net 3D geometry possible; better fatigue at dynamic joints; ~5% share

alternatives.name: Status quo — continue machining from bar stock
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero acquisition overhead; 40-60% material swarf waste; high machining cost per part; no geometry optimization

jobSteps.description: Specify profile cross-section geometry, dimensional tolerances, material grade, surface hardness, and certification requirements for boom/arm/frame components
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Profile geometry specification determines die design feasibility and near-net-shape advantage
jobSteps.rawStatement: Specify profile cross-section geometry, dimensional tolerances, material grade, surface hardness, and certification requirements for boom/arm/frame components
jobSteps.rationale: Profile geometry specification determines production feasibility and die design

jobSteps.description: Identify and pre-qualify suppliers capable of producing the specified profile cross-section in required grade, tolerance, and hardness
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Profile supplier pool for custom cross-sections is narrow; qualification is effort-intensive
jobSteps.rawStatement: Identify and pre-qualify suppliers capable of producing the specified profile cross-section in required grade, tolerance, and hardness
jobSteps.rationale: Profile supplier pool for custom cross-sections is narrow; qualification is effort-intensive

jobSteps.description: Submit DXF drawing for die design; receive first-article profiles; validate dimensional compliance, hardness, and material certificate
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Die qualification is the primary barrier step for near-net-shape profile supply
jobSteps.rawStatement: Submit DXF drawing for die design; receive first-article profiles; validate dimensional compliance, hardness, and material certificate
jobSteps.rationale: Die qualification is the primary barrier step for near-net-shape profile supply

jobSteps.description: Approve first-article inspection, material certificate, and PPAP-equivalent documentation before serial supply
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Certification integrity is critical — slideway dimensional errors propagate to machine accuracy
jobSteps.rawStatement: Approve first-article inspection, material certificate, and PPAP-equivalent documentation before serial supply
jobSteps.rationale: Certification integrity is critical — dimensional errors propagate to machine accuracy

jobSteps.description: Receive production batch; incoming dimensional, hardness, and straightness inspection; release to machining or direct assembly
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Lot-level compliance is the execution risk; pre-hardened profiles go to assembly, not heat treatment
jobSteps.rawStatement: Receive production batch; incoming dimensional, hardness, and straightness inspection; release to machining or direct assembly
jobSteps.rationale: Lot-level compliance is the execution risk; pre-hardened profiles go to assembly, not heat treatment

jobSteps.description: Track lot-to-lot dimensional, hardness, and straightness consistency across ongoing deliveries
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Drift causes silent machine accuracy degradation across multiple machines
jobSteps.rawStatement: Track lot-to-lot dimensional, hardness, and straightness consistency across ongoing deliveries
jobSteps.rationale: Drift causes silent machine accuracy degradation across multiple machines
